title: Add warning on conflicting multiple assignments
body:
I use Verilator for linting as `verilator --lint-only -Wall myModule.v`. 
Linting following code does not produce any warnings (I am using yosys afterwards and it is happy to process it too)
```
reg some;
assign some = 1;
assign some = 0;
```
not only this is confusing, the effect of this code depends on assignment order. That is in the code above `some` is always 1, while in the code below it is always 0
```
reg some;
assign some = 0;
assign some = 1;
```
The first assignment takes precedence in yosys. But in my case it was source of a hard to find bug because assignments were far away from each other. I found similar ticket, although that one is not for continuous assignments #1939 (I think it should have been a feature request instead).

So the questions are: what is expected behavior here, should this be reported by linter, and if not what is recommended way to handle this?

I use 
```
verilator --version
Verilator 4.016 2019-06-16 rev UNKNOWN_REV
```
built from 18f8cd05 (master).
